sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 37

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 37:1
items.html:
(to the question) Vav Ze'ira [small Vav]
Tifferet is called the great Vav, or a Vav with a head [Rosh], because it is the level of Bechina Aleph, comprised of CHaGaT NHY, where the CHaGaT are the discernment of KaCHaB of Hasadim [mercies] — its Rosh; therefore Tifferet is discerned as a Vav with a Rosh. But Yesod is the discernment of the level of the Light of Malchut — that is, the level of NHY lacking CHaGaT; therefore it is discerned as a small Vav without a Rosh, for it lacks the CHaGaT, which are the secret of the Rosh. (p. 766, Item 50)
